name: shelly
description:
The common octopus is a highly intelligent, active predator.
They are masters of camouflage and will change colour and texture depending on their mood or the situation.
Most octopuses are pretty inactive during the daytime
trumpet fish is a long-bodied marine fish commonly found in tropical coral reefs

name: juliet
description: a tiny yellowish fish known for its long snout (requested by tony)

koi carps are ornamental, domesticated variants of common carp, originating in 19th-century Japan.
They are popular in ponds and can reach 3 feet in length
Starfish, or sea stars, are marine invertebrates closely related to sea urchins.
Sea urchins are spherical marine invertebrates who live on the ocean seabed
Jellyfish are ancient, spineless invertebrates composed of 95–98% water
seahorse is a small, unique marine fish found in shallow tropical waters, characterized by a horse-like head
Clownfish are small, vibrant saltwater fish known for their symbiotic relationship with sea anemones in coral reefs
Surgeonfish are a family of about 75–80 species of vibrant, tropical marine fish, commonly found in coral reefs
Butterflyfish are small, brightly colored, tropical fish, typically under 8 inches long, known for their thin bodies

The Napoleon fish is one of the largest and most distinctive fish inhabiting Indo-Pacific coral reefs
they are immediately recognizable by a jiggly lump on their forehead, thick lips, and vibrant colors
zebra turkeyfish is a small, venomous scorpionfish native to the Indo-Pacific ocean
Blowfish are tropical marine fish famous for inflating their bodies with water or air when threatened to deter predators

Pufferfish are uniquely shaped, slow-moving fish found in warm, shallow waters
Known for their ability to ingest large amounts of water or air to inflate into a ball when threatened
Anchovies are small saltwater fish known for their slender bodies and silvery-blue color
The horse mackerel is a slender fish found in the Atlantic and Mediterranean, typically measuring 20–60 cm
The barred knifejaw is a marine fish recognized by seven distinct vertical black bars on a white body
sea bass...it's at least a C+
The red snapper is a deep-red saltwater fish found in the Gulf of Mexico known for its pointed face and red eyes
the common dab is a small saltwater fish known for having both eyes on the right side of it's body
olive flounder is a small saltwater fish known for having both eyes on the left side of it's body
Tuna are fast-swimming, migratory fish found in tropical and temperate oceans worldwide
The blue marlin is a massive, fast-swimming predatory fish found in warm Atlantic waters
The bitterling is a small carplike freshwater fish native to Europe and Asia
the pale chub is a small freshwater fish native to East Asian rivers
the crucian carp is a medium-sized freshwater fish native to Europe and Asia
the common dace is a small freshwater fish native to Europe and Asia
Freshwater gobies are small, bottom-dwelling fish known for having modified pelvic fins that act as a suction cup to cling to rocks in swift currents
Loaches are a diverse group of small, bottom-dwelling freshwater fish native to Eurasia and Africa
The bluegill is a pan-shaped freshwater sunfish native to North America, known for its small mouth, olive-green color and a distinct, dark blue-black "ear" flap
The yellow perch is a small, freshwater fish native to North America, easily recognized by its golden-yellow body
Black bass are popular freshwater fish native to North America
Tilapia is a fast-growing and highly adaptable freshwater fish native to Africa and the Middle East
The pike is a large, slender freshwater fish known as a fierce apex predator, commonly found in lakes, rivers, and canals
sweetfish is a small, migratory freshwater fish native to East Asia
The cherry salmon is a small salmon species typically native to East Asian rivers and the Northwest Pacific
the arctic char is a cold-water salmonid fish closely related to salmon and trout, commonly found in high-latitude northern lakes and coastal waters
The California golden trout is a vibrant, small salmonid native to high-elevation streams in California’s southern Sierra Nevada
The Stringfish is a rare, high-value fish in Animal Crossing: New Horizons, selling for 15,000 Bells :)

Salmon are ray-finned fish known for their silver-scaled bodies and anadromous life cycle
 — meaning they are born in fresh water, travel to the ocean, and return to fresh water to spawn
The guppy is a small, vibrant, and highly popular tropical freshwater fish, known for its extreme color variations and ease of care
The nibble fish is a small, toothless freshwater fish native to Western Asia, commonly known for its use in skin-exfoliating spa pedicures
Angelfish are deep-bodied tropical fish known for their triangular profiles with flowing fins
Betta fish are small, vibrant freshwater fish native to Southeast Asia
The neon tetra is a popular, small and peaceful freshwater fish from South America, known for its vibrant iridescent blue and red horizontal stripes
Rainbowfish are a diverse group of small, colorful freshwater fish native to Australia, New Guinea, and Indonesia, popular iridescent scales
Piranhas are small-to-medium-sized freshwater fish native to South American rivers, known for their razor-sharp triangular teeth, and powerful jaws
The Arowana is a group of large, predatory freshwater fish recognized for their prehistoric appearance
Mahi-mahi is a vibrant fish found in tropical and subtropical oceans, famous for their dazzling golden-green and blue colors
The arapaima is one of the world's largest freshwater fish, native to the Amazon River basin
Sturgeons are ancient fish, often called "living fossils," that have existed for over 200 million years

Jellyfish are ancient marine invertebrates made of over 95% water
they lack brains, hearts, or bones and can range from 1cm to 2 meters in size

name: momo
description:
The tiger shark is a large predator found in tropical and coastal waters
recognizable by dark, vertical stripes that fade with age, these sharks can grow up to 18+ feet in length

name: yuzu
description: The lemon shark is a large yellow-brown coastal shark found in tropical Atlantic and Pacific waters

name: alfredo
description: The blacknose shark is a small species of shark found in tropical coastal waters. Recognized by a dusky smudge on its nose

name: leo
description: The leopard shark is a small coastal shark easily recognized by its silvery-bronze body covered in dark spots

name: monica
description:
The pyjama shark is a small catshark native to South Africa's coastal waters
It is easily identified by 5–7 long, dark stripes running down its body

name: pete
description: Hammerhead sharks are predatory sharks recognized by their flattened, T-shaped heads, known as a cephalofoil
Snails are small gastropod mollusks characterized by a soft, slimy body and a coiled, protective shell

name: axel
description: The axolotl is a critically endangered aquatic salamander native only to the Xochimilco lake complex near Mexico City

The king penguin is the world's second-largest penguin species
standing 85–95 cm tall with yellow upper chest markings. Inhabiting subantarctic islands

The Green Turtle is a large, endangered sea turtle found in tropical/subtropical seas
They measure up to 4 feet in length and weigh over 300 pounds
The flatback turtle is a medium-sized marine turtle native to coastal waters of Northern Australia and Papua New Guinea

name: gandalf
description: The blue-ringed octopus is a tiny, highly venomous marine cephalopod found in Pacific/Indian Ocean tide pools

name: coco
description: The coconut octopus is a small, highly intelligent Indo-Pacific cephalopod famous for using tools

name: louisa
description: The Caribbean reef squid is a small cephalopod found in shallow Western Atlantic coral reefs

name: philip
description: Bobtail squid are small cephalopods, often called "dumpling"

name: filip
description:
The cownose ray is a species of eagle ray known for its distinct snout that resembles a cow's nose
Primarily found in the Western Atlantic Ocean and Gulf of Mexico
The Ocellate River Stingray is a freshwater fish native to South American river basins
